markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: **GTA VI's confirmed November 19, 2026 release date anchors the multi-outcome Polymarket, with traders pricing events like GPT-6, new Rihanna or Playboi Carti albums, and geopolitical shifts near even odds ahead of the Rockstar launch.** Two prior delays—to May then November—have fixed the timeline, while Take-Two's May 2026 earnings call reaffirmed no further slips, setting the stage for marketing to ramp up post-World Cup. High-volume outcomes such as a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or Drake's Iceman reflect speculation on rapid cultural or tech milestones in the compressed window, with resolution hinging on official Rockstar announcements and verifiable public releases before the game hits PS5 and Xbox Series X. Market-implied odds capture crowd wisdom on unpredictable entertainment cycles, where album rollouts and AI model launches can accelerate or stall based on industry momentum.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Trader sentiment on the "Harvey Weinstein prison time?" market heavily favors "No Prison Time" (81.3% implied probability) due to repeated prosecutorial setbacks in New York and Weinstein’s advanced age and declining health.** 

The disgraced producer’s original 2020 New York conviction was overturned on appeal, leading to multiple retrials on charges tied to accuser Jessica Mann. The most recent trial ended in a mistrial with a hung jury in mid-May 2026, marking the second consecutive deadlock on the key rape count. Prosecutors have until late June to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial. Weinstein, now 74 and reportedly dealing with cancer, diabetes, and recent heart issues, has been held at Rikers Island throughout these proceedings. 

A separate 2022 California conviction carries a 16-year sentence that was set to run consecutively, yet traders appear to price in the high likelihood of release or plea resolutions that avoid further meaningful incarceration, especially given the pattern of jury skepticism and his physical condition. Upcoming decisions on retrial or any potential plea discussions remain the key near-term catalysts that could shift these market-implied odds.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France leads the World Cup winner market at 17.6% implied probability amid a tightly bunched top tier, reflecting recent shifts where Les Bleus overtook Spain in many outright odds thanks to exceptional squad depth anchored by Kylian Mbappé and a history of deep tournament runs. Spain sits at 14.1% after Euro 2024 success and strong recent form but faces scrutiny over Lamine Yamal’s fitness entering the expanded 48-team group stage. Portugal, England, and defending champions Argentina follow closely at 10.8%, 10.5%, and 9.3% respectively, sustained by Cristiano Ronaldo’s leadership, Thomas Tuchel’s England setup, and Lionel Messi’s potential swan-song campaign. This competitiveness stems from multiple elite European and South American sides boasting comparable talent, recent major-tournament pedigree, and favorable draws, leaving limited separation among the frontrunners as the tournament opens.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination market at 24.6% due to his high national profile as California governor, recent memoir release, populist shifts on issues like AI regulation, and role as a prominent Trump critic through media appearances and podcasts. The wide-open field reflects the absence of formal candidate announcements or binding primary polling two years before the cycle intensifies. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez at 9.3% draws support from progressive activists, while Jon Ossoff at 8.3% and others like Kamala Harris benefit from Senate experience or prior national visibility. Key differentiators include executive records versus legislative or ideological appeal, with consolidation likely hinging on 2026 midterm outcomes, early endorsements, and voter coalition-building in battleground states.
